description: Pins the identity of the canonical Sinclair ZX Spectrum 48K ROM as an OPAQUE artifact (ADR-0024): the machine maps these exact 16384 bytes at 0x0000-0x3FFF and executes them, but the DNA specifies no ROM-routine behaviour (no rom-entry-points.md). Only the LD-BYTES tape-load entry 0x0556 is referenced, by the tape edge-load slice (W10.10). The vendored blob is verified by size + sha256 so a regeneration loads the same ROM and drift is caught.

license.terms: Amstrad permits free, unmodified redistribution of the 48K ZX Spectrum ROM as part of an emulator provided this copyright notice is retained; commercial use requires written permission.
license.notice: The 48K ZX Spectrum ROM is copyright Amstrad plc, redistributed by their kind permission.

source.name: Sinclair ZX Spectrum 48K ROM
source.summary: The original 16K BASIC/firmware ROM of the 48K machine. Mined once from the project oracle (packages/emulator/rom/48k.rom) during reconstruction and vendored DNA-side; treated as an opaque blob per ADR-0024.

publish: Vendored under dna/conformance/ for the tape edge-load integration oracle (W10.10) and this identity check only. Per ADR-0024, published package tarballs should resolve the ROM from a user-supplied path rather than vendoring it.
